name: Great Vero Loop Trail
name_original: Gran Vuelta al Vero – circular por Alquézar, Lecina y el Santuario de la Nuez
description: This difficult 27.3-mile mountain biking route through Sierra de Guara offers challenging terrain, hike-a-bike sections, and stunning canyon
region_intro: Mountain biking around Colungo offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guara Natural Park, characterized by limestone massifs, deep canyons, and ancient paths. The region's unique pre-Pyrenean geography provides a mix of challenging enduro routes and more relaxed trails. Riders can expect varied surfaces, including rocky sections and loose gravel, often following historical livestock and trade routes. This landscape provides a setting for mountain biking across different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Colungo The most popular mountain bike trail is Somontano de Barbastro – circular a San Pelegrín desde Alquézar, a 16.5 miles (26.6 km) moderate route that takes about 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the area around Alquézar, known for its historic architecture and proximity to canyons. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Great Vero Loop Trail, a difficult 27.3 miles (44.0 km) path. This extensive loop follows the River Vero, offering views of the cultural park known for its rock shelters. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Alquézar loop from Alquézar, an 11.9 miles (19.2 km) moderate trail leading through scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Colungo is defined by limestone canyons, varied terrain, and ancient paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Colungo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Colungo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Colungo?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails around Colungo, providing a wide range of options for different skill levels within the Sierra de Guara Natural Park.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of mountain bike trails around Colungo?
A: The trails around Colungo cater to all skill levels. You'll find a mix of routes, with the majority being moderate (around 29 trails), some challenging difficult routes (7 trails), and a few easier options (1 trail) suitable for less experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Colungo?
A: The terrain in Colungo is diverse, characterized by limestone massifs, deep canyons, and ancient paths. Riders can expect varied surfaces, including rocky sections, conglomerate gravel, and loose gravel, often following historical livestock and trade routes. This unique pre-Pyrenean geography offers both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for families in Colungo?
A: While many trails offer varied terrain, some of the moderate routes can be suitable for families with older children or those with some mountain biking experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alquézar loop from Alquézar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.9-mile trail that offers scenic views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails around Colungo?
A: Many natural parks in Spain, including Sierra de Guara, generally allow dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check specific local regulations or signage for individual routes to ensure a dog-friendly experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore dramatic canyons like the Palomeras and Gargantas, and even see the Devil's Bridge in the Fornocal canyon. Many routes offer views of the cultural park known for its rock shelters, such as those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977161611/great-vero-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Vero Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Historic villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2980198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alquézar&lt;/a&gt; and ancient olive trees are also notable sights.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Colungo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Colungo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977156672/somontano-de-barbastro-circular-a-san-pelegrin-desde-alquezar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somontano de Barbastro – circular a San Pelegrín desde Alquézar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Gregorio – Albarda Medieval Bridge loop from Alquézar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Colungo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best seasons for mountain biking in Colungo and the Sierra de Guara due to milder temperatures and more pleasant conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder weather, though the region is rideable year-round.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting mountain bike trails in Colungo?
A: Colungo and nearby villages like Alquézar offer various parking options, often in designated areas within or on the outskirts of the settlements. Many trailheads are accessible from these parking spots, making it convenient to start your ride.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails in Colungo?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in the Sierra de Guara can be limited. It's often more practical to travel by car to Colungo or a nearby village and then access the trails. Some local bus services might connect larger towns in the region, but direct access to remote trail starts is rare.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Colungo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Colungo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes of the Sierra de Guara, and the unique experience of riding ancient paths through canyons and historical areas.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Colungo offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977161611/great-vero-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Vero Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a demanding 27.3-mile path with significant elevation changes, ideal for those looking for an extensive and rigorous ride.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails in Colungo?
A: Colungo itself, and nearby villages such as Alquézar, offer local cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. These establishments often serve traditional Aragonese cuisine and provide a welcoming atmosphere for visitors.

Q: Are there any specific cultural or historical sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in cultural heritage. Many trails pass through or near historic villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2980198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alquézar&lt;/a&gt;, known for its medieval architecture. You can also find ancient churches and hermitages, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1208930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Gregorio&lt;/a&gt;, along various routes. The area is also famous for its prehistoric cave paintings, with the Cave Art Visitor Centre in Colungo serving as a starting point for exploration.
tips: One of the reference towns of the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara natural park. The town has been declared a historical-artistic complex and is considered one of the most beautiful towns in Spain.

The hermitage of San Gregorio, is located in the upper part of the town, approximately one kilometer away, which constitutes a magnificent viewpoint over the medieval town of Alquézar and Guara Somontano; In addition to being a great place to have a picnic as it is equipped with benches and tables, it is located in the heart of the Somontano Nature Trail.

Legend has it that when sawing a walnut tree that was in this place, an image of the virgin appeared with a branch loaded with walnuts, and that is why this hermitage was built for her. The image was later looted during the Civil War and has been replaced by a modern replica.

This majestic holm oak, 16.5 m tall and 28 m in diameter, was named European Tree of the Year 2021 after having also been awarded at regional and state level. It is affectionately called "La Castañera" because of the number of acorns it produced in the harshest winters, up to 600 kilos.

The oak has its own legend that you can read here: https://www.huescalamagia.es/blog/la-carrasca-de-lecina/
Impressive gorge formed by the Vero river where you can also see many vultures that nest in the caves of the imposing walls.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Gran Vuelta al Vero route?
faqs.answer: This is an exacting route that typically takes mountain bikers around 5 to 6 hours to complete. The total duration listed on komoot is 4 hours 47 minutes, but this can vary significantly based on skill level and stops.

faqs.question: Is the Gran Vuelta al Vero route suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It features challenging terrain, including rocky singletrack, loose stones, and significant climbing and descending. There are also demanding 'hike-a-bike' sections where you'll need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Gran Vuelta al Vero?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. Approximately 52% consists of dirt roads, 42% is singletrack, and a smaller portion (7%) is on asphalt. Expect rocky singletrack descents, loose stones, and technical sections that require strong bike handling skills and core strength.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Gran Vuelta al Vero route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through stunning landscapes and notable landmarks. Highlights include the historic village and Alquézar Castle, the Hermitage of San Gregorio, and the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Walnut. You'll also see the impressive La Carrasca de Lecina and ride through the dramatic Vero Gorge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular panoramic views throughout the ride. The route offers breathtaking vistas of the Vero and Balced canyons, as well as the entire Pyrenees mountain range. You'll also pass through picturesque villages like Alquézar and enjoy 'gorgeous walled paths' leading to Betorz and Lecina, with impressive rock formations carved by the Vero River.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Gran Vuelta al Vero route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and typically starts and ends in Alquézar. You can find parking options within or near the village of Alquézar, which serves as a convenient starting point for this loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guara region can get very hot in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking this demanding route, avoiding the extreme heat of July and August and potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: The Gran Vuelta al Vero route is located within the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara. While there are generally no specific entrance fees for the natural park itself, it's always advisable to check local regulations for mountain biking, especially concerning protected areas or specific trails. No specific permits are typically required for this route, but always respect park rules.

faqs.question: Is the Gran Vuelta al Vero trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara can vary. While dogs are often allowed on trails, they typically must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always check the most current park guidelines before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Alquézar and Lecina, where you may find opportunities to purchase food and water. However, given the demanding nature and length of the ride, it's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration, especially during warmer months, as services might not always be conveniently located or open.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Gran Vuelta al Vero route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Almazorre - Betorz - Almazorre, Lecina - Betorz/Lecina - Fuente de Lecina, and Paúles de Sarsa - Santa María de la Nuez - Tozal de Asba trails. It also briefly touches the Camino Natural del Somontano de Barbastro and the Sendero Histórico Variante Sur.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Walnut
